prerequisites: An OpenWeather account. Sign up with your email at openweathermap.org to receive an API key (appid).
An active OpenWeather API key (appid). Your key is listed under the 'API keys' tab on your account page.
Geographic coordinates for the location you want to track: a latitude (lat) and longitude (lon) in decimal degrees. Both are required for every request.

authMethods.name: API key (appid)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate by passing your OpenWeather API key as the appid parameter on every request. This is the connector's single secret field. Obtain the key from the 'API keys' tab of your OpenWeather account page.

setupSteps.title: Create an OpenWeather account and get your API key
setupSteps.body: Register at openweathermap.org using your email address. After signing up, your API key (appid) is available under the 'API keys' tab on your account page, where you can also generate additional keys.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and required configuration
setupSteps.body: Enter your API key in the appid field (required, secret). Set lat (latitude) and lon (longitude) for the target location; both are required decimal coordinates.

setupSteps.title: Set optional configuration
setupSteps.body: Optionally set units to control measurement output (standard for Kelvin, metric for Celsius, or imperial for Fahrenheit; standard is the default). Optionally set lang to localize text output to a supported language code (for example en, fr, de, es). Optionally set only_current to control whether the connector retrieves only current weather data versus the full response.

sources.title: How to get an OpenWeather API key (APPID)
